get.description: Query users by a set of filters.

schema.description: Filter users by phone number in E.164 format.
The maximum number of users to return.
The user ID to start the query from. Use this for pagination by passing the `lastEvaluatedUserId` from the previous response.

patch.description: Update information about a user. Any fields which are omitted in the request will be left unchanged.

custom.description: A JSON object which can include any key/value pairs. Should be provided when using rules based on your own data points.

username.description: The username used as the primary identifier for passkeys, e.g. an email address.

displayName.description: The display name used as an optional secondary identifier for passkeys, e.g. the user's full name.

emailVerified.description: Set this to true if the user's email address has already been verified in your own system. This will result in any email OTP or magic link authenticators being updated to reflect the new email address, and the next email challenge will be sent to the new email address.

phoneNumber.description: The user's phone number in E.164 format.

phoneNumberVerified.description: Set this to true if the user's phone number has already been verified in your own system. This will result in any SMS authenticators being updated to reflect the new number and the next SMS challenge will be sent to the new phone number.

defaultVerificationMethod.description: The default verification method to use for the user. If not provided, the default verification method will be the first verification method which the user has enrolled.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a user and all their associated data, including all of their authenticators and actions. This operation is asynchronous and may take a few minutes to complete after the request returns.

get.description: Gets a list of the authenticators configured for the tenant.
Gets a list of the user's currently enrolled authenticators.

post.description: Enroll an authenticator on behalf of a user. This operation should only be used in cases where you have already verified a user's email address or phone number in your own system.

verificationMethod.description: The verification method of the authenticator being enrolled.

email.description: The user's email address. Required for EMAIL_MAGIC_LINK and EMAIL_OTP.

phoneNumber.description: The user's phone number in E.164 format. Required for SMS.

otpUri.description: The formatted OTP URI. Required for AUTHENTICATOR_APP.

credentialId.description: The passkey credential ID from the WebAuthn credential. Required for PASSKEY.

credentialPublicKey.description: The passkey credential public key (base64url-encoded). Required for PASSKEY.

counter.description: The signature counter from the WebAuthn authenticator. Used for PASSKEY to detect cloned credentials.

name.description: A display name for the passkey authenticator (e.g. device or browser name). Used for PASSKEY.

isDefault.description: Whether this authenticator should be set as the user's default.

post.description: Record authentication events performed by users and initiate challenges via Authsignal's pre-built UI or Authsignal Client SDKs.

redirectUrl.description: The URL which the pre-built UI will redirect back to after the user exits Authsignal's pre-built UI. Only required when using the pre-built UI in redirect mode.

redirectToSettings.description: If set to true, the user will be shown the authentication settings screen after completing a challenge. Use this flag to allow users to manage their own authenticators through Authsignal's pre-built UI.

phoneNumber.description: The user's phone number in E.164 format.

ipAddress.description: The user's IP address. Should be provided when using rules based on location or other IP-derived features.

userAgent.description: The user agent identifying a browser or app. Should be provided when using rules based on device.

deviceId.description: An ID which identifies the user's device. Should be provided when using rules based on device.

scope.description: The scopes granted to the pre-built UI and the token which can be passed to Client SDKs. By default the only scope is `read:authenticators`.

custom.description: A JSON object which can include any key/value pairs. Should be provided when using rules based on custom data points from your own app.

customDomain.description: The custom domain to use for the pre-built UI. If not provided, the default domain will be used.

url.description: The URL for initiating a challenge using Authsignal's pre-built UI. You can redirect to this URL if the state determines that a challenge is required, or if you want to allow the user to enroll or to manage their existing authenticator settings.

token.description: A short-lived token which can be passed to Authsignal's Client SDKs (e.g. when using passkeys) or to authenticate to Authsignal's client API.

isEnrolled.description: True if the user is enrolled with at least one verification method and can be challenged.

idempotencyKey.description: A unique key which identifies a particular action. This key can be used to determine if the user has successfully completed a challenge.

allowedVerificationMethods.description: The list of verification methods which the user is permitted to enroll.

enrolledVerificationMethods.description: The list of verification methods which the user has enrolled.

ruleIds.description: The IDs of the triggered rules.

get.description: Get detailed information on a tracked action.

schema.description: A comma-separated list of action codes to include in your query. If omitted, all action codes will be included.
If provided, the query will include actions from this date. Must be specified in ISO 8601 format - e.g. `2020-01-01T00:00:00Z`. The default and maximum value is 30 days ago.

post.description: Initiate a challenge by sending a verification code to an  email address or phone number.

verificationMethod.description: The verification method to use for the challenge.

action.description: The action code used to identify the challenge in the Authsignal Portal.

idempotencyKey.description: The idempotency key for the action. An idempotency key can be used to initiate multiple challenges for the same action.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number in E.164 format. Required if the verification method is 'SMS'.

email.description: The email address. Required if the verification method is 'EMAIL_OTP'.

userId.description: The primary ID of the user in your system. Required when initiating a challenge to enroll or update an authenticator for an existing user.

scope.description: Providing scopes will authorize enrolling or updating an authenticator for an existing user. Must only be provided when initiating a challenge from an already authenticated state.

ipAddress.description: The user's IP address. Can be provided to use rules based on location or other IP-derived features.

userAgent.description: The user agent identifying a browser or app. Can be provided to use rules based on device.

deviceId.description: An ID which identifies the user's device. Can be provided to use rules based on device.

custom.description: A JSON object which can include any key/value pairs. Can be provided to use rules based on your own data points.

idempotencyKey.description: The idempotency key for the action. An idempotency key can be used to initiate multiple challenges for the same action.

expiresAt.description: The challenge expiry as a Unix timestamp in seconds.

post.description: Verify an SMS or email-based challenge by submitting a verification code.

verificationCode.description: The verification code submitted by the user.

isVerified.description: True if the verification code is valid.

verificationMethod.description: The verification method used for the challenge if successful.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number for which the challenge was initiated. Only present if verification is successful and the verification method is 'SMS' or 'WHATSAPP'.

email.description: The email address for which the challenge was initiated. Only present if verification is successful and the verification method is 'EMAIL_OTP'.

post.description: Claim an SMS or email-based challenge on behalf of a user, associating it with an ID for a user record in your system.

skipVerificationCheck.description: If set to true, the requirement to call Verify Challenge before claiming the challenge on behalf of a user will be skipped. This option may be used in scenarios where an OTP code has been verified independently of Authsignal. It should be used with caution and could introduce security risks if used incorrectly. Defaults to false.

token.description: A short-lived token which can be used subsequently in your integration to validate that the challenge was completed successfully by the user.

verificationMethod.description: The verification method used to complete the challenge.
The verification method used to complete the challenge. This field can be used to determine if the challenge has been successfully completed and which method was used.

post.description: Validate the result of an action using a token obtained after redirecting back from the pre-built UI or returned by an Authsignal SDK.

token.description: The token obtained after a redirect or returned by an Authsignal SDK.

userId.description: The ID of the user (if known). If passed, this will be used to validate that the token is for the correct user.

action.description: The action which was tracked. Use this to ensure that the action being validated matches the action that was tracked.

stateUpdatedAt.description: The time in ISO 8061 format when the state of the action was last updated.

post.description: Create a session for a user based on a client token obtained after successfully completing a challenge. This will return an access token and refresh token which can be used to manage a session.

token.description: The client token obtained after completing a challenge.

clientId.description: The ID of the app client for which the access token and refresh token should be issued.

post.description: Validate a session for a given access token. This will ensure both that the token signature is valid and that the token has not been revoked.

accessToken.description: The access token to be validated.

clientIds.description: A list of IDs of the app clients for which the access token is valid. If provided, the access token must be issued for one of these clients.

user.description: A JSON object containing user claims.

expiresAt.description: The token expiry as a Unix timestamp in seconds.

verificationMethod.description: The verification method which was used to create the session.

post.description: Refresh a session for a given refresh token. This will return a new access token and refresh token, revoking any previously issued tokens.

refreshToken.description: The refresh token to be exchanged for a new access token and refresh token.

post.description: Revoke a session for a given access token.

accessToken.description: The access token to be revoked.

post.description: Revoke all sessions for a given user.

204.description: The user's sessions were successfully revoked.

get.description: Get a list of devices associated with a user.

post.description: Invalidate a user's device, treating it as if it were a new device on subsequent actions.

basicAuth.description: Use your Authsignal Server API secret key as the username and leave the password empty. The secret key can be found in the API Keys section of the Authsignal Portal settings page.

action.name: action
action.description: A short human-readable code which defines the action that the user is performing, e.g. `signIn`. This value will be displayed in the Authsignal Portal and can be used to configure rules for authentication events with differing risk profiles. Values are validated with the following regex: `^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{(1, 64)}$`.

userAuthenticatorId.name: userAuthenticatorId
userAuthenticatorId.description: The ID of the user authenticator.

idempotencyKey.name: idempotencyKey
idempotencyKey.description: The unique key which identifies the particular action which was tracked.

ActionState.description: The current state of the action.

accessToken.description: An access token which can be used to authenticate requests.

refreshToken.description: A refresh token which can be exchanged for a new access token and refresh token.

custom.description: The custom data passed as input when tracking the action.

ActionOutput.description: The output produced by the rules engine when determining if the action requires a challenge.

createdAt.description: The time in ISO 8061 format when the action was first created via track.

stateUpdatedAt.description: The time in ISO 8061 format when the state of the action was last updated.

verifiedByAuthenticatorId.description: The ID of the authenticator which was used to complete the challenge associated with the action.

priorityRuleId.description: The ID of the rule that had the highest priority and determined the `state` of the user action.

ruleIds.description: The IDs of the triggered rules.

rules.description: A snapshot of the triggered rules at the time the user action was tracked, ordered by priority.

idempotencyKey.description: The idempotency key for the user action.

createdAt.description: The time in ISO 8061 format when the action was first created.

updatedAt.description: The time in ISO 8061 format when the action was last updated.

stateUpdatedAt.description: The time in ISO 8061 format when the state of the action was last updated.

verifiedByAuthenticatorId.description: The ID of the authenticator which was used to complete the challenge associated with the action.

userAgent.description: The user agent provided when the action was tracked.

ipAddress.description: The IP address provided when the action was tracked.

countryCode.description: The country code derived from the IP address if provided.

createdAt.description: The time in ISO 8061 format when the action was first created via track.

stateUpdatedAt.description: The time in ISO 8061 format when the state of the action was last updated.

ruleIds.description: The IDs of the triggered rules.

authenticatorId.description: The ID of the authenticator configuration.

isActive.description: Determines whether the authenticator configuration is currently active/enabled.

isEditableByUser.description: Determines whether the user is able to edit the authenticator in the pre-built UI.

isHiddenToUser.description: Determines whether the authenticator in hidden in the pre-built UI.

smsChannel.description: For SMS authenticator configurations only. If set to `DEFAULT`, only regular SMS is allowed. If set to `WHATSAPP`, both regular SMS and WhatsApp messages are allowed.

previousSmsChannel.description: The channel that was last used by the user to complete an SMS challenge. Only defined if the verification method is SMS.

name.description: The name of the passkey. Defaults to `aaguidMapping.name` if available.
The name of the credential manager where the passkey is stored e.g. 'iCloud Keychain'.

svgIconLight.description: A base64 encoded light variant SVG icon for the credential manager.

svgIconDark.description: A base64 encoded dark variant SVG icon for the credential manager.

deviceName.description: The name of the device (e.g. 'iPhone 16 Pro'). Only used for PUSH verification methods.

isEnrolled.description: True if the user is enrolled with at least one verification method and can be challenged.

phoneNumber.description: The user's phone number in E.164 format.

username.description: The username used as the primary identifier for passkeys, e.g. an email address.

displayName.description: The display name used as an optional secondary identifier for passkeys, e.g. the user's full name.

allowedVerificationMethods.description: The list of verification methods which the user is permitted to enroll.

enrolledVerificationMethods.description: The list of verification methods which the user has enrolled.

custom.description: A JSON object which can include any key/value pairs. Should be provided when using rules based on your own data points.

phoneNumber.description: The user's phone number in E.164 format.

username.description: The username used as the primary identifier for passkeys, e.g. an email address.

displayName.description: The display name used as an optional secondary identifier for passkeys, e.g. the user's full name.

custom.description: A JSON object which can include any key/value pairs. Should be provided when using rules based on your own data points.

success.description: `true` if the user was deleted successfully.

users.description: The list of users matching the query.

lastEvaluatedUserId.description: The user ID to use as the starting point for the next page of results. If not present, there are no more results.

phoneNumber.description: The user's phone number in E.164 format.

username.description: The username used as the primary identifier for passkeys, e.g. an email address.

displayName.description: The display name used as an optional secondary identifier for passkeys, e.g. the user's full name.

isEnrolled.description: True if the user is enrolled with at least one verification method and can be challenged.

enrolledVerificationMethods.description: The list of verification methods which the user has enrolled.

createdAt.description: The time when the user was created.

Locale.description: The locale of the user in BCP 47 format. Used to localize the pre-built UI, email, and SMS messages.

createdAt.description: The time when the device was first created.

authenticatedAt.description: The time when the device was last authenticated.

invalidatedAt.description: The time when the device was invalidated.

lastVerificationMethod.description: The verification method last used on this device.
